rawText:
Donald Trump's strategy in Iran has been compared to Florida weather -- wait five minutes and it changes -- as the president veered, within a single weekend, from declaring that the war was "winding down" and that America did not even use the Strait of Hormuz, to issuing a 48-hour ultimatum threatening to "hit and obliterate" Iran's power plants, a whiplash that his defenders call strategic ambiguity but that nearly everyone else recognises as the flailing of a presidency that entered this war without a plan for ending it.

The first of Trump's four options, negotiation, appears the least plausible, not only because Iran has been attacked twice while engaged in talks with America and is understandably reluctant to try again, but because its leadership is in such disarray -- with the new supreme leader Mojtaba Khamenei unseen since assuming the role, the most pragmatic interlocutor Ali Larijani assassinated, and even the choice of mediator contested after Oman angered Gulf neighbours with its sympathetic stance -- that no one can be certain who truly speaks for the regime or possesses the authority to make concessions.

The second option, declaring victory and walking away, would be the most Trumpian manoeuvre of all, and the president has done it before -- claiming in June that Iran's nuclear programme had been "obliterated," only to describe that same programme as a mortal threat eight months later -- but this time the fiction would be harder to sustain, because Iran would retain roughly 400 kilograms of near-bomb-ready uranium, a chokehold on the strait, and a demonstrated willingness to use energy as a weapon, leaving Gulf allies furious and the half-century-old American commitment to ensuring the flow of Persian Gulf oil in ruins.

The war is eroding Trump's three political superpowers -- his ability to impose his own reality on events, his remorseless use of leverage, and his dominion over the Republican Party -- because oil markets keep score in a way that no amount of rhetorical embellishment can override: with Brent crude spiking above $110, American petrol and diesel prices up sharply from inauguration levels, and Iran signalling that it will grant selective passage through Hormuz to friendly nations, it is the regime in Tehran, not the White House, that is accumulating bargaining chips.

The third option, staying the course for several more weeks of air strikes, appeals to Israeli officials who note that daily Iranian missile-and-drone launches have fallen from roughly 1,000 on the war's first day to fewer than 100, but this trajectory offers no guaranteed endpoint, for as long as Iran can sustain even sporadic attacks on shipping, it can keep the strait functionally closed -- and each passing day depletes the air-defence interceptors on which Israel and the Gulf states depend, as demonstrated when two ballistic missiles penetrated Israeli defences and injured more than 160 people.

Escalation, the fourth path -- what Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent euphemistically termed "escalate to de-escalate" -- could involve seizing Kharg island, where 90% of Iran's oil exports are loaded, dispatching commandos to secure enriched uranium, or following through on the threat to destroy Iran's electrical grid, yet each of these gambles risks triggering precisely the counter-escalation it is meant to forestall, including Iranian strikes on the desalination and power plants upon which Gulf populations depend for survival.

What makes Trump's predicament structurally intractable is that none of the four options is guaranteed to end the war: he could declare victory only for Iran to keep Hormuz closed for months as a punitive measure; he could press on through April and find himself at the same impasse; and he could escalate dramatically, only to discover that seizing an island or bombing a power grid does not compel a regime that has already absorbed thousands of strikes to capitulate -- a dilemma rooted in the original miscalculation that a show of overwhelming force would produce swift submission.

Republican support for the war, though still nominally strong, is softening beneath the surface, with a vocal MAGA faction led by Tucker Carlson openly speaking of betrayal, elected Republicans seething in private over Trump's refusal to heed warnings about the strait, and the party's prospects in the November midterms deteriorating so sharply that the chances of losing the Senate have risen by ten points to roughly even odds -- a political erosion that, should it accelerate, would leave Trump a lamer duck than any second-term president in modern memory.

The deeper danger, as the war grinds on without resolution, is not merely that Trump will lose politically but that a weakened and cornered president will become a more reckless one -- lashing out at NATO allies, cutting Ukraine loose to punish Europe, demanding tribute from Japan and South Korea, threatening media outlets that criticise the campaign, escalating fights with the Federal Reserve, or meddling in the midterm elections themselves, all in an attempt to recapture the aura of dominance on which his political identity depends.

History may record the Iran war as the moment when the central contradiction of Trumpism -- the belief that raw power, whether economic or military, can bend any adversary to submission without the need for strategy, allies, or an exit plan -- collided with an opponent willing to absorb enormous punishment in order to demonstrate, at catastrophic cost to itself and the world, that leverage runs in more than one direction.

overview.title: Why None of Trump's Options in Iran Look Good

sentences.original: Donald Trump's strategy in Iran has been compared to Florida weather -- wait five minutes and it changes -- as the president veered, within a single weekend, from declaring that the war was "winding down" and that America did not even use the Strait of Hormuz, to issuing a 48-hour ultimatum threatening to "hit and obliterate" Iran's power plants, a whiplash that his defenders call strategic ambiguity but that nearly everyone else recognises as the flailing of a presidency that entered this war without a plan for ending it.
sentences.translation: 트럼프의 이란 전략은 플로리다 날씨에 비유되어 왔다 -- 5분만 기다리면 바뀐다 -- 대통령이 한 주말 사이에 전쟁이 "마무리 국면"이며 미국은 호르무즈 해협을 사용하지도 않는다고 선언하다가, 이란의 발전소를 "타격하고 소멸시키겠다"는 48시간 최후통첩을 발부하는 것으로 급선회했기 때문이다. 이 급변을 옹호자들은 전략적 모호성이라 부르지만, 거의 모든 사람은 종전 계획 없이 전쟁에 뛰어든 대통령직의 허우적거림으로 인식한다.

grammarElements.text: Donald Trump's strategy in Iran
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: has been compared to Florida weather
grammarElements.label: 수동태 현재완료

grammarElements.text: as the president veered from ... to ...
grammarElements.label: 이유 부사절 (from A to B)

grammarElements.text: a whiplash that his defenders call ... but that nearly everyone else recognises as
grammarElements.label: 동격 명사 + 이중 관계절 (대조)

vocabulary.meaning: 급격한 방향 전환, 채찍질 같은 충격
vocabulary.example: The policy whiplash left investors uncertain about the government's true intentions.
The campaign's flailing in its final weeks cost it the election.

expressions.usage: 입장이나 정책이 극단적으로 빠르게 바뀔 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Subject] has been compared to [vivid analogy] -- [punchline] -- as [evidence of erratic behaviour]
patterns.explanation: 생생한 비유로 도입한 뒤 구체적 증거로 뒷받침하는 저널리즘 도입 패턴
examples: The government's messaging has been compared to a weather vane -- it points wherever the wind blows -- as ministers contradicted each other three times in a single day.
The company's strategy has been compared to a pinball -- bouncing from wall to wall -- as management reversed course on its core product twice in one quarter.

drillQuestions.question: Trump's strategy in Iran has been compared to Florida weather -- wait five minutes and it changes -- a ______ that his defenders call strategic ambiguity.

sentences.notes: 플로리다 날씨 비유로 시작해 독자의 관심을 끌고, whiplash와 flailing이라는 신체적 은유로 정책의 혼란을 생생하게 전달한다.

koreanAlignment.en: has been compared to Florida weather
koreanAlignment.ko: 플로리다 날씨에 비유되어 왔다

koreanAlignment.en: the president veered from declaring ... to issuing
koreanAlignment.ko: 대통령이 선언하다가 발부하는 것으로 급선회했다

koreanAlignment.en: a whiplash that his defenders call strategic ambiguity
koreanAlignment.ko: 옹호자들이 전략적 모호성이라 부르는 급변

koreanAlignment.en: the flailing of a presidency that entered this war without a plan
koreanAlignment.ko: 계획 없이 전쟁에 뛰어든 대통령직의 허우적거림

sentences.original: The first of Trump's four options, negotiation, appears the least plausible, not only because Iran has been attacked twice while engaged in talks with America and is understandably reluctant to try again, but because its leadership is in such disarray -- with the new supreme leader Mojtaba Khamenei unseen since assuming the role, the most pragmatic interlocutor Ali Larijani assassinated, and even the choice of mediator contested after Oman angered Gulf neighbours with its sympathetic stance -- that no one can be certain who truly speaks for the regime or possesses the authority to make concessions.
sentences.translation: 트럼프의 네 가지 선택지 중 첫 번째인 협상은 가장 가능성이 낮아 보인다. 이란이 미국과 대화 중에 두 차례 공격받았으므로 다시 시도하기를 꺼리는 것은 당연하기 때문만이 아니라, 지도부가 극심한 혼란에 빠져 있기 때문이다 -- 새 최고지도자 모즈타바 하메네이는 취임 이래 모습을 드러내지 않았고, 가장 실용적인 대화 상대 알리 라리자니는 암살당했으며, 오만이 동정적 입장으로 걸프 이웃국들을 분노케 한 이후 중재자 선택마저 논쟁 중이라 -- 누가 진정으로 정권을 대변하는지, 양보할 권한을 가진 사람이 누구인지 아무도 확신할 수 없다.

grammarElements.text: The first of Trump's four options, negotiation
grammarElements.label: 주어 + 동격

grammarElements.text: appears the least plausible
grammarElements.label: 동사 + 보어

grammarElements.text: not only because ... but because
grammarElements.label: 이중 이유절

grammarElements.text: in such disarray that no one can be certain
grammarElements.label: so/such ... that 결과절

vocabulary.example: The explanation sounded plausible but lacked supporting evidence.

expressions.usage: 조직이나 지도부가 제대로 기능하지 못하는 상태를 묘사할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Option] appears the least plausible, not only because [reason A], but because [reason B] is in such disarray that [consequence]
patterns.explanation: 선택지의 비현실성을 이중 이유와 결과절로 논증하는 패턴
examples: A diplomatic solution appears the least plausible, not only because trust has collapsed, but because the opposition is in such disarray that no negotiating partner can deliver on commitments.
Reform appears the least plausible, not only because the ruling party opposes it, but because the bureaucracy is in such disarray that implementation would be impossible.

drillQuestions.question: The first of Trump's four options, negotiation, appears the least ______, not only because Iran has been attacked twice while engaged in talks.

sentences.notes: 네 가지 선택지를 순서대로 분석하는 구조의 첫 번째. not only ... but because의 이중 논증과 such ... that 결과절이 협상 불가능성을 체계적으로 입증.

koreanAlignment.en: The first of Trump's four options, negotiation
koreanAlignment.ko: 트럼프의 네 가지 선택지 중 첫 번째인 협상

koreanAlignment.en: appears the least plausible
koreanAlignment.ko: 가장 가능성이 낮아 보인다

koreanAlignment.en: its leadership is in such disarray
koreanAlignment.ko: 지도부가 극심한 혼란에 빠져 있다

koreanAlignment.en: who truly speaks for the regime or possesses the authority to make concessions
koreanAlignment.ko: 누가 진정으로 정권을 대변하고 양보할 권한을 가졌는지

sentences.original: The second option, declaring victory and walking away, would be the most Trumpian manoeuvre of all, and the president has done it before -- claiming in June that Iran's nuclear programme had been "obliterated," only to describe that same programme as a mortal threat eight months later -- but this time the fiction would be harder to sustain, because Iran would retain roughly 400 kilograms of near-bomb-ready uranium, a chokehold on the strait, and a demonstrated willingness to use energy as a weapon, leaving Gulf allies furious and the half-century-old American commitment to ensuring the flow of Persian Gulf oil in ruins.
sentences.translation: 두 번째 선택지인 승리를 선언하고 떠나는 것은 가장 트럼프다운 기동이 될 것이며, 대통령은 전에도 그렇게 한 적이 있다 -- 6월에 이란의 핵 프로그램이 "소멸되었다"고 주장해 놓고 8개월 뒤 같은 프로그램을 치명적 위협으로 묘사한 바 있다 -- 하지만 이번에는 그 허구를 유지하기가 더 어려울 것이다. 이란이 약 400킬로그램의 거의 무기급 우라늄, 해협에 대한 목줄, 에너지를 무기로 사용할 의지를 입증한 채 남아 있어, 걸프 동맹국들은 분노하고 페르시아만 석유의 흐름을 보장하겠다는 반세기의 미국 약속은 폐허에 놓이게 될 것이기 때문이다.

grammarElements.text: The second option, declaring victory and walking away
grammarElements.label: 주어 + 동격 동명사구

grammarElements.text: would be the most Trumpian manoeuvre of all
grammarElements.label: 가정법 동사 + 보어

grammarElements.text: claiming ... only to describe
grammarElements.label: 분사구문 + only to (예상 밖 결과)

grammarElements.text: leaving Gulf allies furious and the commitment in ruins
grammarElements.label: 결과 분사구문

vocabulary.example: The political manoeuvre caught the opposition off guard.

expressions.usage: 이전 행동과 모순되는 후속 행동을 드러낼 때 사용하는 아이러니 표현

patterns.pattern: [Option] would be the most [characteristic] manoeuvre of all, and [actor] has done it before -- [past example] -- but this time [why it fails]
patterns.explanation: 과거 선례를 인정한 뒤 이번에는 통하지 않을 이유를 밝히는 패턴
examples: Blaming the media would be the most predictable manoeuvre of all, and the administration has done it before -- deflecting criticism by attacking journalists -- but this time the evidence is too overwhelming to dismiss.
Cutting interest rates would be the most conventional manoeuvre of all, and the central bank has done it before -- during the 2020 crisis -- but this time inflation makes such a move dangerous.

drillQuestions.question: Declaring victory and walking away would be the most Trumpian ______ of all, and the president has done it before.

sentences.notes: Trumpian이라는 조어가 이 선택지의 성격을 한마디로 규정. only to 구문이 과거 자기모순을 드러내고, but this time으로 현재 상황의 차이를 강조.

koreanAlignment.en: declaring victory and walking away
koreanAlignment.ko: 승리를 선언하고 떠나는 것

koreanAlignment.en: the most Trumpian manoeuvre of all
koreanAlignment.ko: 가장 트럼프다운 기동

koreanAlignment.en: the fiction would be harder to sustain
koreanAlignment.ko: 그 허구를 유지하기가 더 어려울 것이다

koreanAlignment.en: leaving Gulf allies furious and the commitment in ruins
koreanAlignment.ko: 걸프 동맹국들은 분노하고 약속은 폐허에 놓이게 하며

sentences.original: The war is eroding Trump's three political superpowers -- his ability to impose his own reality on events, his remorseless use of leverage, and his dominion over the Republican Party -- because oil markets keep score in a way that no amount of rhetorical embellishment can override: with Brent crude spiking above $110, American petrol and diesel prices up sharply from inauguration levels, and Iran signalling that it will grant selective passage through Hormuz to friendly nations, it is the regime in Tehran, not the White House, that is accumulating bargaining chips.
sentences.translation: 이 전쟁은 트럼프의 세 가지 정치적 초능력을 잠식하고 있다 -- 사건에 자기만의 현실을 부과하는 능력, 가차 없는 지렛대 활용, 공화당에 대한 지배력 -- 유가 시장은 어떤 수사적 미화로도 무효화할 수 없는 방식으로 점수를 매기기 때문이다. 브렌트유가 110달러를 넘어 급등하고, 미국의 휘발유와 경유 가격이 취임 시점 대비 크게 오른 가운데, 이란이 우호국에 호르무즈 선택적 통과를 허용하겠다고 시사하면서, 협상 카드를 축적하고 있는 것은 백악관이 아니라 테헤란의 정권이다.

grammarElements.text: The war
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: is eroding
grammarElements.label: 현재진행 동사

grammarElements.text: Trump's three political superpowers
grammarElements.label: 목적어 (세 가지 병렬)

grammarElements.text: it is the regime in Tehran, not the White House, that is accumulating
grammarElements.label: 강조 구문 (it is ... that)

vocabulary.example: The report was full of embellishment and short on facts.

expressions.usage: 시장이나 현실이 수사와 달리 객관적 결과를 드러낼 때 사용하는 은유

patterns.pattern: [Event] is eroding [actor]'s [strengths], because [objective measure] keeps score in a way that [rhetoric] cannot override
patterns.explanation: 수사와 현실의 괴리를 드러내며 객관적 지표가 거짓말을 허용하지 않음을 보여주는 패턴
examples: The recession is eroding the government's credibility, because unemployment figures keep score in a way that campaign slogans cannot override.
The scandal is eroding the CEO's authority, because stock prices keep score in a way that corporate messaging cannot override.

drillQuestions.question: The war is eroding Trump's three political superpowers because oil markets keep score in a way that no amount of rhetorical ______ can override.

sentences.notes: superpowers라는 대중문화적 은유와 keep score라는 스포츠 은유가 정치 분석에 생동감을 더한다. it is ... that 강조 구문으로 권력 이동의 방향을 확정.

koreanAlignment.en: The war is eroding Trump's three political superpowers
koreanAlignment.ko: 전쟁은 트럼프의 세 가지 정치적 초능력을 잠식하고 있다

koreanAlignment.en: oil markets keep score in a way that no rhetorical embellishment can override
koreanAlignment.ko: 유가 시장은 수사적 미화로 무효화할 수 없는 방식으로 점수를 매긴다

koreanAlignment.en: it is the regime in Tehran, not the White House, that is accumulating bargaining chips
koreanAlignment.ko: 협상 카드를 축적하는 것은 백악관이 아니라 테헤란의 정권이다

sentences.original: The third option, staying the course for several more weeks of air strikes, appeals to Israeli officials who note that daily Iranian missile-and-drone launches have fallen from roughly 1,000 on the war's first day to fewer than 100, but this trajectory offers no guaranteed endpoint, for as long as Iran can sustain even sporadic attacks on shipping, it can keep the strait functionally closed -- and each passing day depletes the air-defence interceptors on which Israel and the Gulf states depend, as demonstrated when two ballistic missiles penetrated Israeli defences and injured more than 160 people.
sentences.translation: 세 번째 선택지인 수 주간 더 공습을 지속하는 것은 이란의 일일 미사일 및 드론 발사가 개전 첫날 약 1,000발에서 100발 미만으로 줄었다고 지적하는 이스라엘 관리들에게 매력적이지만, 이 궤적은 확실한 종점을 보장하지 못한다. 이란이 해운에 대한 산발적 공격만이라도 유지할 수 있는 한 해협을 기능적으로 폐쇄 상태로 둘 수 있기 때문이며 -- 매일이 지날수록 이스라엘과 걸프 국가들이 의존하는 방공 요격체가 고갈된다는 것은 두 발의 탄도미사일이 이스라엘 방어망을 뚫고 160명 이상을 부상시켰을 때 입증되었다.

grammarElements.text: The third option, staying the course
grammarElements.label: 주어 + 동격 동명사구

grammarElements.text: appeals to
grammarElements.label: 동사

grammarElements.text: but this trajectory offers no guaranteed endpoint
grammarElements.label: 대조절 (but)

grammarElements.text: for as long as Iran can sustain even sporadic attacks
grammarElements.label: 이유+조건절 (for as long as)

vocabulary.example: Sporadic gunfire continued throughout the night.

expressions.usage: 어려운 상황에서도 현재 전략을 바꾸지 않고 계속 밀고 나가는 것을 묘사할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Option] appeals to [supporters who cite evidence], but this trajectory offers no guaranteed endpoint, for as long as [condition persists]
patterns.explanation: 선택지의 매력을 인정한 뒤 구조적 한계를 밝히는 양면 분석 패턴
examples: The lockdown appeals to health officials who cite falling case numbers, but this trajectory offers no guaranteed endpoint, for as long as the virus mutates, restrictions may return.
The tariff strategy appeals to advisers who cite improved trade figures, but this trajectory offers no guaranteed endpoint, for as long as China retaliates, costs will rise.

drillQuestions.question: This trajectory offers no guaranteed endpoint, for as long as Iran can sustain even ______ attacks on shipping.

sentences.notes: 세 번째 선택지 분석. appeals to로 매력을 인정하되 but으로 즉시 반전. for as long as 조건절이 구조적 교착의 논리를 명확히 제시.

koreanAlignment.en: The third option, staying the course for several more weeks of air strikes
koreanAlignment.ko: 세 번째 선택지인 수 주간 더 공습을 지속하는 것

koreanAlignment.en: this trajectory offers no guaranteed endpoint
koreanAlignment.ko: 이 궤적은 확실한 종점을 보장하지 못한다

koreanAlignment.en: as long as Iran can sustain even sporadic attacks on shipping
koreanAlignment.ko: 이란이 산발적 공격만이라도 유지할 수 있는 한

koreanAlignment.en: each passing day depletes the air-defence interceptors
koreanAlignment.ko: 매일이 지날수록 방공 요격체가 고갈된다

sentences.original: Escalation, the fourth path -- what Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent euphemistically termed "escalate to de-escalate" -- could involve seizing Kharg island, where 90% of Iran's oil exports are loaded, dispatching commandos to secure enriched uranium, or following through on the threat to destroy Iran's electrical grid, yet each of these gambles risks triggering precisely the counter-escalation it is meant to forestall, including Iranian strikes on the desalination and power plants upon which Gulf populations depend for survival.
sentences.translation: 네 번째 길인 확전 -- 재무장관 스콧 베센트가 완곡하게 "확전을 통한 긴장 완화"라고 명명한 것 -- 은 이란 석유 수출의 90%가 선적되는 카르그 섬을 점령하거나, 특수부대를 파견해 농축 우라늄을 확보하거나, 이란의 전력망을 파괴하겠다는 위협을 실행하는 것을 포함할 수 있다. 그러나 이 도박들 각각은 바로 그것이 방지하려는 역확전을 촉발할 위험이 있으며, 여기에는 걸프 인구가 생존을 위해 의존하는 담수화 및 발전 시설에 대한 이란의 타격이 포함된다.

grammarElements.text: Escalation, the fourth path
grammarElements.label: 주어 + 동격

grammarElements.text: what Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent euphemistically termed
grammarElements.label: 삽입 관계절

grammarElements.text: could involve
grammarElements.label: 가능성의 조동사 + 동사

grammarElements.text: yet each of these gambles risks triggering precisely the counter-escalation it is meant to forestall
grammarElements.label: 대조절 (yet) + 아이러니

vocabulary.example: The layoffs were euphemistically described as a workforce optimisation.

expressions.usage: 더 강한 군사 행동으로 상대를 굴복시켜 분쟁을 끝낸다는 전략 개념을 설명할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Option] could involve [action A], [action B], or [action C], yet each risks triggering precisely the [outcome] it is meant to forestall
patterns.explanation: 여러 행동 가능성을 나열한 뒤 모두 역효과를 낳을 수 있음을 밝히는 패턴
examples: The crackdown could involve arrests, censorship, or martial law, yet each risks triggering precisely the uprising it is meant to forestall.
The intervention could involve sanctions, asset freezes, or travel bans, yet each risks triggering precisely the capital flight it is meant to forestall.

drillQuestions.question: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent ______ termed the strategy 'escalate to de-escalate.'

sentences.notes: euphemistically가 정책 명칭의 기만성을 폭로하고, precisely the counter-escalation it is meant to forestall이 확전 논리의 자기모순을 한 구절에 압축.

koreanAlignment.en: Escalation, the fourth path
koreanAlignment.ko: 네 번째 길인 확전

koreanAlignment.en: what Bessent euphemistically termed 'escalate to de-escalate'
koreanAlignment.ko: 베센트가 완곡하게 '확전을 통한 긴장 완화'라고 명명한 것

koreanAlignment.en: each of these gambles risks triggering precisely the counter-escalation it is meant to forestall
koreanAlignment.ko: 이 도박들 각각은 방지하려는 바로 그 역확전을 촉발할 위험이 있다

koreanAlignment.en: desalination and power plants upon which Gulf populations depend for survival
koreanAlignment.ko: 걸프 인구가 생존을 위해 의존하는 담수화 및 발전 시설

sentences.original: What makes Trump's predicament structurally intractable is that none of the four options is guaranteed to end the war: he could declare victory only for Iran to keep Hormuz closed for months as a punitive measure; he could press on through April and find himself at the same impasse; and he could escalate dramatically, only to discover that seizing an island or bombing a power grid does not compel a regime that has already absorbed thousands of strikes to capitulate -- a dilemma rooted in the original miscalculation that a show of overwhelming force would produce swift submission.
sentences.translation: 트럼프의 곤경을 구조적으로 풀 수 없게 만드는 것은 네 가지 선택지 중 어느 것도 전쟁 종결을 보장하지 못한다는 점이다. 승리를 선언해도 이란이 징벌적 조치로 호르무즈를 수개월 폐쇄해 놓을 수 있고, 4월까지 밀어붙여도 같은 교착에 빠질 수 있으며, 극적으로 확전해도 이미 수천 발의 타격을 흡수한 정권에 섬 점령이나 전력망 폭격이 항복을 강제하지 못한다는 것을 발견할 뿐이다 -- 압도적 무력의 과시가 신속한 복종을 낳을 것이라는 원래의 오판에 뿌리를 둔 딜레마이다.

grammarElements.text: What makes Trump's predicament structurally intractable
grammarElements.label: 명사절 주어 (What절)

grammarElements.text: is that none of the four options is guaranteed to end the war
grammarElements.label: 동사 + 보어 that절

grammarElements.text: he could ... only for Iran to ... / only to discover
grammarElements.label: 세 가지 가정 + only to 반전 (병렬)

grammarElements.text: a dilemma rooted in the original miscalculation
grammarElements.label: 동격 명사구

vocabulary.example: The border dispute has proved intractable for decades.

expressions.usage: 기대와 결과의 괴리를 드러내는 아이러니 표현

patterns.pattern: What makes [predicament] structurally intractable is that none of [options] is guaranteed to [goal]
patterns.explanation: 문제의 구조적 해결 불가능성을 선언하는 분석적 도입 패턴
examples: What makes the housing crisis structurally intractable is that none of the proposed solutions is guaranteed to bring prices down without triggering a recession.
What makes the refugee crisis structurally intractable is that none of the diplomatic channels is guaranteed to produce a ceasefire.

drillQuestions.question: What makes Trump's predicament structurally ______ is that none of the four options is guaranteed to end the war.

sentences.notes: 기사의 핵심 논지를 담은 문장. What절로 시작해 네 선택지 모두의 실패 가능성을 세 가지 가정으로 순회한 뒤, original miscalculation으로 근본 원인을 지적.

koreanAlignment.en: What makes Trump's predicament structurally intractable
koreanAlignment.ko: 트럼프의 곤경을 구조적으로 풀 수 없게 만드는 것

koreanAlignment.en: none of the four options is guaranteed to end the war
koreanAlignment.ko: 네 가지 선택지 중 어느 것도 전쟁 종결을 보장하지 못한다

koreanAlignment.en: only to discover that seizing an island does not compel a regime to capitulate
koreanAlignment.ko: 섬 점령이 정권에 항복을 강제하지 못한다는 것을 발견할 뿐이다

koreanAlignment.en: a dilemma rooted in the original miscalculation
koreanAlignment.ko: 원래의 오판에 뿌리를 둔 딜레마

sentences.original: Republican support for the war, though still nominally strong, is softening beneath the surface, with a vocal MAGA faction led by Tucker Carlson openly speaking of betrayal, elected Republicans seething in private over Trump's refusal to heed warnings about the strait, and the party's prospects in the November midterms deteriorating so sharply that the chances of losing the Senate have risen by ten points to roughly even odds -- a political erosion that, should it accelerate, would leave Trump a lamer duck than any second-term president in modern memory.
sentences.translation: 전쟁에 대한 공화당의 지지는 명목상으로는 여전히 강하지만 표면 아래에서 약해지고 있다. 터커 칼슨이 이끄는 목소리 큰 MAGA 파벌이 공개적으로 배신을 말하고, 선출직 공화당 의원들은 해협에 관한 경고를 무시한 트럼프에 사석에서 부글부글 끓고 있으며, 11월 중간선거에서의 당의 전망이 급격히 악화되어 상원을 잃을 확률이 10포인트 올라 거의 반반이 되었다 -- 이것이 가속화될 경우 트럼프를 현대 기억 속 어떤 재선 대통령보다도 더한 레임덕으로 만들 정치적 침식이다.

grammarElements.text: Republican support for the war
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: though still nominally strong
grammarElements.label: 양보 삽입구

grammarElements.text: is softening beneath the surface
grammarElements.label: 동사구

grammarElements.text: with a vocal MAGA faction ... elected Republicans ... and the party's prospects ...
grammarElements.label: with + 세 가지 독립분사 (병렬)

vocabulary.example: Voters were seething over the government's handling of the crisis.

expressions.usage: 공식 입장과 실제 분위기가 다를 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Support], though still nominally strong, is softening beneath the surface, with [evidence A], [evidence B], and [evidence C]
patterns.explanation: 표면적 강세와 실질적 약화의 괴리를 세 가지 증거로 뒷받침하는 패턴
examples: Consumer confidence, though still nominally high, is softening beneath the surface, with retail spending slowing, credit card balances rising, and household savings falling to post-pandemic lows.

Allied unity, though still nominally intact, is softening beneath the surface, with France breaking ranks on sanctions, Germany delaying arms deliveries, and eastern European nations demanding more reassurance.

drillQuestions.question: Republican support for the war, though still nominally strong, is softening beneath the surface, with elected Republicans ______ in private over Trump's refusal to heed warnings.

sentences.notes: nominally와 beneath the surface의 대비가 공화당 내부 균열의 본질을 포착. with 분사구문 세 개의 병렬이 증거를 체계적으로 나열.

koreanAlignment.en: Republican support, though still nominally strong, is softening beneath the surface
koreanAlignment.ko: 공화당의 지지는 명목상 강하지만 표면 아래에서 약해지고 있다

koreanAlignment.en: a vocal MAGA faction led by Tucker Carlson openly speaking of betrayal
koreanAlignment.ko: 터커 칼슨이 이끄는 MAGA 파벌이 공개적으로 배신을 말하고

koreanAlignment.en: elected Republicans seething in private
koreanAlignment.ko: 선출직 공화당 의원들이 사석에서 부글부글 끓고 있다

koreanAlignment.en: a lamer duck than any second-term president in modern memory
koreanAlignment.ko: 현대 기억 속 어떤 재선 대통령보다 더한 레임덕

sentences.original: The deeper danger, as the war grinds on without resolution, is not merely that Trump will lose politically but that a weakened and cornered president will become a more reckless one -- lashing out at NATO allies, cutting Ukraine loose to punish Europe, demanding tribute from Japan and South Korea, threatening media outlets that criticise the campaign, escalating fights with the Federal Reserve, or meddling in the midterm elections themselves, all in an attempt to recapture the aura of dominance on which his political identity depends.
sentences.translation: 전쟁이 해결 없이 질질 끌리면서 더 깊은 위험은, 트럼프가 정치적으로 패배하는 것만이 아니라, 약해지고 궁지에 몰린 대통령이 더 무모한 대통령이 된다는 것이다 -- 나토 동맹국에 맹공을 퍼붓고, 유럽을 벌하기 위해 우크라이나를 버리고, 일본과 한국에 조공을 요구하고, 작전을 비판하는 언론사를 위협하고, 연준과의 싸움을 확대하거나, 중간선거 자체에 개입하는 것 -- 모두 자신의 정치적 정체성이 의존하는 지배력의 아우라를 되찾으려는 시도이다.

grammarElements.text: The deeper danger
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: is not merely that ... but that
grammarElements.label: 동사 + not merely A but B 보어절

grammarElements.text: lashing out ... cutting ... demanding ... threatening ... escalating ... or meddling
grammarElements.label: 여섯 가지 동명사 병렬

grammarElements.text: all in an attempt to recapture the aura of dominance
grammarElements.label: 목적 부사구

vocabulary.example: A reckless foreign policy could drag the country into another conflict.

expressions.usage: 분쟁이나 문제가 고통스럽게 장기화되는 상태를 묘사할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: The deeper danger is not merely that [obvious risk] but that [greater risk] -- [list of reckless actions], all in an attempt to [underlying motive]
patterns.explanation: 명백한 위험 뒤에 숨은 더 큰 위험을 드러내고 구체적 행동 목록으로 뒷받침하는 패턴
examples: The deeper danger is not merely that sales will fall but that a panicking board will slash R&D -- cancelling projects, firing researchers, and abandoning long-term strategy, all in an attempt to meet next quarter's earnings target.

The deeper danger is not merely that the party will lose the election but that a cornered leader will weaponise state institutions -- purging civil servants, stacking courts, and censoring media, all in an attempt to cling to power.

drillQuestions.question: The deeper danger is that a weakened and cornered president will become a more ______ one.

sentences.notes: not merely A but B 구조로 진짜 위험이 정치적 패배가 아니라 권력의 무모한 행사임을 밝힌다. 여섯 개의 동명사 병렬이 공포의 목록을 구축.

koreanAlignment.en: The deeper danger, as the war grinds on without resolution
koreanAlignment.ko: 전쟁이 해결 없이 질질 끌리면서 더 깊은 위험은

koreanAlignment.en: not merely that Trump will lose politically but that a weakened president will become a more reckless one
koreanAlignment.ko: 정치적 패배만이 아니라 약해진 대통령이 더 무모해진다는 것

koreanAlignment.en: lashing out at NATO allies, cutting Ukraine loose, demanding tribute
koreanAlignment.ko: 나토 동맹국에 맹공, 우크라이나를 버림, 조공 요구

koreanAlignment.en: all in an attempt to recapture the aura of dominance
koreanAlignment.ko: 지배력의 아우라를 되찾으려는 시도

sentences.original: History may record the Iran war as the moment when the central contradiction of Trumpism -- the belief that raw power, whether economic or military, can bend any adversary to submission without the need for strategy, allies, or an exit plan -- collided with an opponent willing to absorb enormous punishment in order to demonstrate, at catastrophic cost to itself and the world, that leverage runs in more than one direction.
sentences.translation: 역사는 이란 전쟁을 트럼프주의의 핵심 모순이 -- 경제적이든 군사적이든 날것의 힘이 전략도, 동맹도, 출구 계획도 없이 어떤 적수든 복종시킬 수 있다는 믿음이 -- 자신과 세계에 재앙적 대가를 치르면서까지 지렛대가 한 방향으로만 작용하지 않는다는 것을 보여주려는 상대와 충돌한 순간으로 기록할지도 모른다.

grammarElements.text: History
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: may record
grammarElements.label: 가능성의 조동사 + 동사

grammarElements.text: the Iran war as the moment when
grammarElements.label: 목적어 + 시간 관계절

grammarElements.text: the central contradiction of Trumpism collided with an opponent willing to absorb enormous punishment
grammarElements.label: 관계절 내 주절

vocabulary.example: Small nations can sometimes find unexpected leverage in trade negotiations.

expressions.phrase: runs in more than one direction
expressions.usage: 힘이나 영향력이 일방적이지 않고 양방향임을 지적할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: History may record [event] as the moment when [belief/ideology] collided with [reality/opponent]
patterns.explanation: 역사적 의미를 부여하며 이념과 현실의 충돌을 선언하는 결론 패턴
examples: History may record the pandemic as the moment when the myth of efficiency collided with the reality of fragility.
History may record the financial crisis as the moment when the dogma of self-regulating markets collided with systemic risk.

drillQuestions.question: History may record the Iran war as the moment when the central contradiction of Trumpism collided with an opponent willing to demonstrate that ______ runs in more than one direction.

sentences.notes: 기사의 결론이자 트럼프주의에 대한 역사적 평가. 삽입된 이중 대시가 핵심 모순을 정의하고, leverage runs in more than one direction이 전체 기사의 논지를 한 문구로 요약한다.

koreanAlignment.en: History may record the Iran war as the moment when
koreanAlignment.ko: 역사는 이란 전쟁을 ~의 순간으로 기록할지도 모른다

koreanAlignment.en: the central contradiction of Trumpism
koreanAlignment.ko: 트럼프주의의 핵심 모순

koreanAlignment.en: the belief that raw power can bend any adversary to submission
koreanAlignment.ko: 날것의 힘이 어떤 적수든 복종시킬 수 있다는 믿음

koreanAlignment.en: without the need for strategy, allies, or an exit plan
koreanAlignment.ko: 전략도, 동맹도, 출구 계획도 없이

koreanAlignment.en: leverage runs in more than one direction
koreanAlignment.ko: 지렛대가 한 방향으로만 작용하지 않는다
